seoTitle: Change Your Logo and Favicon in GoHighLevel
metaDescription: Update your GoHighLevel logo and favicon to build a consistent brand identity across your website, funnels, and client portal.
intro: A consistent logo and favicon across your GoHighLevel account help create a professional, trustworthy brand experience for clients and customers. This tutorial covers uploading a new logo, replacing the favicon, and confirming the changes display correctly everywhere they should.

steps.heading: Open branding settings
steps.body: Navigate to the branding section of your GoHighLevel account where logo and favicon options are managed.

steps.heading: Upload your logo and favicon
steps.body: Add your new logo and favicon images, following recommended dimensions and formats for the best results.

steps.heading: Save changes and preview branding
steps.body: Save your updates and preview how the new branding appears before rolling it out fully.

steps.heading: Verify branding appears correctly
steps.body: Check your website, funnels, and client portal to confirm the new logo and favicon display consistently across each area.

steps.heading: Apply best practices for professional branding
steps.body: Follow tips for logo and favicon sizing and format to maintain a polished, professional experience for your clients.

faq.question: Where do I change my logo and favicon in GoHighLevel?
faq.answer: Both are managed through the account's branding settings.

faq.question: What image sizes work best for a logo and favicon?
faq.answer: The tutorial covers best practices for logo dimensions, favicon sizes, and supported image formats.

faq.question: Will the new branding show up in my client portal too?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial covers ensuring changes appear correctly across your website, funnels, and client portal.

faq.question: Is this a good first step when setting up a new account?
faq.answer: Yes, the tutorial describes this as one of the first customization steps you should complete for brand consistency.

faq.question: Who is this tutorial for?
faq.answer: Agencies, marketers, business owners, web designers, freelancers, and GoHighLevel beginners.
